title: [Bug]: Bug-report sync accepts non-standard JSON constants
summary: The managed bug-report sync tool can parse local bug JSON containing non-standard constants such as NaN or Infinity when those values appear in ignored extra fields.
observed: A bug report with an ignored NaN field can pass validation even though the source file is not standards-compliant JSON.
expected: Bug-report sync should reject non-standard JSON constants at parse time regardless of whether the field is later used.
reproduction: Run the focused regression test that adds an ignored NaN field to a bug report JSON document and calls load_bug_report.
impact: Bug-report source files are public workflow inputs. Strict parsing keeps test evidence, acceptance criteria, and release-gated closure deterministic.
delivery_semantics: No runtime delivery behavior changes. This fix hardens bug-management automation only.
security: The fix must preserve existing secret, URL, and IP-address detection before GitHub comments or issues are published.
acceptance: A focused regression test proves non-standard constants in bug-report JSON are rejected.
Valid bug reports continue to load.
The error message clearly identifies invalid JSON.
Existing bug sync tests and full checks still pass.
tests: Run the focused regression test, bug-report sync tests, and the full local check script.
documentation: Update CHANGELOG.md and bug workflow documentation if public behavior changes.
closeout: Close only after the release containing the fix is published. Include the release tag, regression evidence, verification evidence, and any remaining limitations.
